Okay, let me try to explain this in my own words. So, the term "wabi-sabi" is a concept in Japanese aesthetics,which I think is related to the idea of the balance between the worldly and the self. So,the term is a combination of two Japanese words: "wabi" and"sabi"which roughly translate to"worldly"and"selfish"respectively. So,the conceptisabout maintaining a balance between the external and the internal.So,in Japanese aesthetics, this idea is often used to discuss the harmony between the materialandthe self.So,it'salso related to the idea of the impermanence and the transienceand the idea of the impermanenceand the transience.So,for example,in traditional Japanese art,like a haiku or a ukiyo or a sumi or a sumi,the balance between the natural and the artificial.So,it's also connected to the idea of the "wabi-sabi"in the context of the Zen Buddhism or the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way of the Way
